resources.string: Luna & Natura helps you live in harmony with the lunar cycle. Discover the daily moon phase and get practical advice on when to cut your hair, sow, harvest and prune according to lunar tradition.
This app is distributed free of charge for personal use. Source code is available on GitHub.

The advice provided by this app is based on folk tradition and empirical observations passed down over time. It does not constitute scientific guidance. The author assumes no responsibility for any use made of it.
The new moon is a time of rest and regeneration. Cutting during these days tends to give unsatisfying results — hair grows more slowly and the cut lasts less. Prefer nourishing treatments and masks.
The waxing moon encourages rapid and vigorous growth. Cut today for thicker, stronger hair. Also ideal for coloring — the color turns out more intense and long-lasting.
At the full moon, vital energy is at its peak. Cutting gives luminous, voluminous hair. Coloring is not recommended — hair is more porous and absorbs unevenly.
The waning moon slows growth: the cut lasts longer and length is better maintained. Ideal if you want to keep your current cut. Also great for strengthening and regenerating treatments.
Earth days: the new moon draws energy downward. Good for sowing roots, bulbs and tubers. Avoid harvesting and transplanting — plants have little vitality at the surface.
Fire and air days: sap rises vigorously. Ideal for sowing fruit plants, above-ground vegetables and transplanting. Root vegetables and tubers prefer the waning moon.
The best time to harvest: fruit, vegetables and herbs picked at full moon have more flavor, more juice and keep longer. Sowing fruit plants also gives excellent results.
Earth and water days: energy flows down to the roots. Excellent time to sow carrots, onions, garlic, potatoes and bulbs. Also good for harvesting preserves and produce to dry.
With sap at its lowest, wood cut now is dry, compact and resistant. Ideal time to collect firewood, for drastic pruning and for cuts you don\'t want to stimulate vigorous regrowth.
The waxing moon pushes sap upward: wood cut now contains more moisture and rots sooner. Wait for the waning moon for cuts, major pruning and firewood collection.
At full moon, sap is at its peak — plants bleed heavily when cut and wounds struggle to heal. Firewood collected now is water-rich and burns poorly. Wait a few more days.
Sap is retreating to the roots: wood is compact, the plant suffers less and wounds heal quickly. The best time for major pruning, formative cuts and firewood collection.
